old_claim.note: Bronze Working provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Bronze casting enabled the prow ram used as a trireme's principal weapon, but the timber hull and three-level rowing architecture did not depend on bronze.

source_shape.source_locator: Exhibition text: the Athlit weapon is a 465 kg single bronze casting attached to a warship prow; its vessel was probably a medium quadrireme or three-tier trireme.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The conserved Athlit artifact directly demonstrates a large cast-bronze ram integrated with the wooden prow of a possible three-tier trireme.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The evidence supports bronze as the material technology for the offensive ram, while leaving the timber hull and oar arrangement independently sufficient to define the vessel class.

ontology_before: Bronze working was attached through a generic materials page without identifying what bronze contributed to the ship.
ontology_after: Bronze working is retained only as a bounded enabler of the trireme's ram, not as a prerequisite of its hull or rowing architecture.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved and strengthened by graph invariant 2026-07-16-greek-trireme-boundary: bronze_working remains enabling.
would_reject_if: Artifact analysis shows the relevant trireme rams were not bronze or that the cited ram cannot represent the three-tier warship tradition.
short_reason: The Athlit artifact directly supports a cast-bronze warship ram while keeping bronze distinct from the vessel's timber and oar systems.
